top5Opportunities.statement: Minimize likelihood of heat-number traceability gaps causing notified-body audit failure under EN 81-20
Minimize run-to-run flange width variation across production batches exceeding +-0.05 mm
Minimize delivery delays exceeding 8 weeks for non-standard T-section profile sizes
Minimize likelihood of rail batches failing EN 10204 3.1 chemical-composition requirements
Minimize likelihood of guide rail deflection exceeding EN 81-20 limits under rated load

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of selecting EN50 T-section that fails EN 81-20 deflection limits at rated load and speed
needs.importanceRationale: Incorrect section selection requires rail replacement after hoistway assembly

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of delivery delays exceeding 8 weeks for non-standard T-section profile sizes
needs.importanceRationale: Die library advantage reduces lead time for sizes in existing inventory

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of rail batches failing EN 10204 3.1 mill certificate chemical-composition requirements
needs.importanceRationale: EN 10204 3.1 inspection certificates issued per heat; grade-specific compliance

needs.statement: Increase ability to achieve <=0.3 mm/m plumb and straightness for 10-floor rail run in <4 field hours
needs.importanceRationale: Pre-straightened bars (<=0.2 mm/m) reduce on-site labor

needs.statement: Minimize batch-to-batch rail straightness variation exceeding 0.2 mm/m causing realignment at joints
needs.importanceRationale: Cold-draw die condition monitoring ensures consistent straightness

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of heat-number traceability gaps between mill certificate and physical rail batch causing audit failure
needs.importanceRationale: IATF 16949 mandates full heat-number linkage; EN 10204 3.1 standard practice

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of rail flange width tolerance exceedances >+-0.1 mm causing guide shoe pre-load errors
Minimize run-to-run flange width variation across production batches exceeding +-0.05 mm causing progressive guide shoe pre-load drift
needs.importanceRationale: Die-process consistency + SPC per shipment; hot-rolled/hollow cannot match

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of guide rail deflection exceeding EN 81-20 limits under rated load
Minimize likelihood of guide rail joint failure under emergency braking at 125% rated load per EN 81-20
Minimize likelihood of cabin vibration exceeding A95=75 mg due to rail surface waviness or out-of-straightness
needs.importanceRationale: Ra 0.4-3.2 um surface; <=0.2 mm/m straightness from cold draw

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of undetected rail joint misalignment >0.3 mm causing cabin vibration
needs.importanceRationale: Splice joint documentation + installation torque spec reduce misalignment risk

needs.statement: Minimize total elevator downtime exceeding 48 hours when replacing single damaged rail section
needs.importanceRationale: Pre-cut standard spare section program per OEM fleet
